_comment: Gate 0 test for the linkedin-hq auto-comment feature. Import this into n8n, then: (1) edit the 'Set test post URN' node to paste the URN of one of your OWN recent LinkedIn posts (so a bad test comment can be deleted instantly), (2) click Execute Workflow, (3) check the 'POST comment to LinkedIn' node's output. If you get 201 + a comment URN back, the official API works for our use case and we can build the full auto-comment MVP. If you get 403 PERMISSION_DENIED, we have to pivot. The /v2/me node auto-fetches your person URN so you don't have to figure it out by hand.
name: Gate 0 — LinkedIn comment API test

assignments.name: comment_text
assignments.value: test comment please ignore, deleting in 30 seconds

parameters.content:
## Gate 0 — LinkedIn comment API test

**What this does:** posts a single test comment to LinkedIn via the official `/v2/socialActions` endpoint using the same OAuth credential the Linkedin Parasite workflow already uses for `Create a post`.

**How to run:**
1. Open the **Set test post URN** node
2. Replace `REPLACE_WITH_19_DIGIT_POST_ID` with the 19-digit post id from one of YOUR OWN recent LinkedIn posts
   - Find a post you made → copy its URL → look for `activity-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX-` → that 19-digit number is the post id
   - Use one of YOUR OWN posts so a bad test comment can be deleted by you immediately without anyone seeing it
3. (Optional) Edit the `comment_text` field to something different
4. Click **Execute Workflow** at the bottom
5. Click the **POST comment to LinkedIn** node → switch to the **Output** tab

**Read the result:**
- **statusCode 201** + JSON body with a comment URN → SUCCESS. Tell Claude. We build the full MVP.
- **statusCode 403** with `PERMISSION_DENIED` → the official API rejects us. Tell Claude. We pivot to the manual review page fallback.
- **statusCode 400** with `INVALID_URN` → URN format issue. Try wrapping the post id as `urn:li:share:XXX` or `urn:li:ugcPost:XXX` instead of `urn:li:activity:XXX` and re-run.
- **Anything else** → paste Claude the exact response body and we debug.

**After SUCCESS:** delete the test comment from LinkedIn UI immediately so it doesn't pollute your real engagement. The test comment text is intentionally embarrassing so you remember to delete it.

The `/v2/me` node above auto-fetches your person URN so you don't have to figure it out by hand.

The HTTP Request node has `neverError: true` set, so even on 4xx/5xx the workflow completes without throwing — you can read the actual error response from the Output tab.
